_meta.purpose: Phasepost lexicon for the verbalizer. Two registers, both flat. NOTES: edge-notation handed to the talker — serialized graph (arrows), not prose. FOLD: plain prose shown to the user. The epistemics (surprise ranking, cursor-scoping, replay) live in the mechanics — in which edges are emitted and in what order at what cursor — never in the voice.

registers.notes: Internal, to the talker. Each note is ONE serialized edge or transform: structured internally as {src, rel, tgt, s, conf, type}, rendered to an arrow-string only at the wall. Reads like a researcher's jotted graph, not sentences. The talker parses arrows and writes English; it cannot copy a note as an answer.
registers.fold: External, to the user. Plain prose, selected and ordered by surprise but never announcing it. No edge-notation, no EO codes, no 'your understanding updates'. Just the thing, said well, in surprise order, withholding what isn't true at the cursor.

talker_prompt_contract.no_recognition: Never name the work, author, or genre — recognition replaces reading. The engine's docMetadata may extract a Gutenberg title (e.g. 'Metamorphosis'); the talker must be handed the FILENAME (pg5200.txt), never that title.
talker_prompt_contract.orientation: Give the talker: (1) the document TYPE we think it is (prose narrative / biography / table / ...), (2) its FILE NAME, (3) its total LENGTH (sentences/words). Orientation without recognition — e.g. 'pg5200.txt · prose narrative · 757 sentences'.
talker_prompt_contract.no_guard: Never write a 'do NOT mention X' guard. A guard is a confession the assembler leaked a post-cursor state — fix the assembler, delete the guard. Cursor-scoping (only left-states true at C are emitted) is the only mechanism; the self-check invariant (no ==> right-state s-index past the cursor) is the enforcement. Never rely on a 3B obeying negation.
talker_prompt_contract.spans_win: SPANS are exact quotes, the evidence floor, and outrank NOTES on conflict; always present, always exact.
talker_prompt_contract.operational_not_flattering: Instructions are followable rules ('lean on spans', 'span wins', 'say so when neither covers it'). No 'expert', 'thorough', 'insightful' — flattery inflates length and pushes toward recognition.

note_line_shapes.explicit_void: A --rel--> [void]   (or  X ==> [unnameable]  for an unwitnessed after-state)

note_grammar.single_arrow: --rel--> : one party acts on another and the DIRECTION is the fact. sister --tends--> Gregor.
note_grammar.double_arrow: <--> : the TIE itself is the unit that changes (a reversal is the bond flipping, not one party acting). sister <--> Gregor: tends ==> rejects.
note_grammar.transform_op: ==> : left-state ==> right-state, read as earlier ==> later IN THE READING. Both sides witnessed where possible; an unwitnessed side is [void] / [unnameable], never guessed.
note_grammar.sub_attribute: .facet : target is a facet of an entity, not the entity, so no junk node is minted. Gregor --notices--> sister.speed; chief_clerk --hears--> Gregor.voice = "animal".
note_grammar.relation_labels: PLAIN LANGUAGE ONLY. tends, rejects, hears, binds, severs, reframes. NEVER the EO code (CON, REC, SEG). The arrow exposes graph STRUCTURE to the talker; the EO machinery stays behind the wall. Punctuation may be structural; vocabulary may not be mechanical.
note_grammar.stamp: MANDATORY. (s330+, current) for an ongoing state at/through the cursor, @s201 for a point event. A note with no stamp is malformed — cursor-relativity is the whole point.
note_grammar.tag: OPTIONAL. [central] / [reversal, high] / [turning point] / [minor]. Type + confidence band, from the surprise magnitude.

ARROW_CONSTRUCTION._doc: How to turn a classified event into a directed-arrow note. The assembler reads THREE fields off the event — operator, site row, and whether endpoints resolved — and the line-shape + direction follow. It does NOT memorize 27 recipes; the shape comes from the operator's ARITY (how many nodes it acts on) and the site ROW. note_rel from the cell supplies the relation label; a more specific relation word from the graph (tends, loves, depends-on) overrides the generic note_rel when present.
ARROW_CONSTRUCTION.direction_convention: agent --rel--> patient. The source is who acts; the target is who/what receives. sister --tends--> Gregor = the sister does the tending. This matches the engine's SVO event shape (S=agent, O=patient) and is what makes the surprise machinery legible: who-acts-on-whom is the asymmetry that carries significance. NEVER point the arrow source=topic-of-record; always source=agent.

relational (CON, SEG, SYN).direction: agent --rel--> patient. For a mutual/standing tie with no clear agent (a marriage, a kinship), point from the grammatical subject of the clause that created it; if neither is agentive, it's a candidate for <-->.
relational (CON, SEG, SYN).transform_shape: relational_transform  A <--> B: X ==> Y  — use <--> when the TIE ITSELF reverses (the bond flips sign), not when one party acts once. The reversal is a property of the edge, so both endpoints sit on the left of the arrow and the ==> carries the edge's two states.
relational (CON, SEG, SYN).if_one_endpoint_unresolved: emit the half-edge to a void target: A --rel--> [void]. A half-edge still gives the resolved node relational mass; do NOT drop it.
relational (CON, SEG, SYN).if_both_unresolved: emit nothing (props acting on props — correctly no node).
relational (CON, SEG, SYN).worked: CON-Link, sister tends Gregor, both resolved, ongoing -> `sister --tends--> Gregor (s330+, current)`.  SEG-Link, the bond is cut, reversal over the reading -> `sister <--> Gregor: tends ==> rejects (s330+ ==> s665)`.

existence (NUL, SIG, INS).arity: one node — brings in, surfaces, or marks the absence of a single node
existence (NUL, SIG, INS).INS_default: the node is MINTED. If the clause only asserts the node exists / is made, emit a PROPERTY line: `A: <what it is> @s`. If the clause has the node act on something, that's actually a relational event — route by the verb, not by INS.
existence (NUL, SIG, INS).SIG_default: the node is NOTICED (ephemeral, the behold moment). Usually a directed edge from the noticer to the noticed, often to a facet: `Gregor --notices--> sister.speed @s`. SIG is never logged as enduring identity — it's an arrival into notice, so it reads as a one-time --notices-->/--sees--> edge, not a standing tie.
existence (NUL, SIG, INS).NUL_default: registered absence. Use explicit_void: `A --rel--> [void] @s` (a specific expected thing absent) or, for a pre-distinction blank, a property line `A: lacks <X> @s`. NUL is first-class — emit it; do not silently skip.
existence (NUL, SIG, INS).entity_transform_case: when an existence operator changes WHAT a node is (type-change), use entity_transform: `A: X ==> Y`. The after-state is [unnameable] if the text never names it. Gregor's s14 is this: `Gregor: man ==> [unnameable] @s14`.
existence (NUL, SIG, INS).worked: INS-Entity, Gregor introduced -> `Gregor: a travelling salesman @s1`.  SIG-Entity, Gregor notices the sister's speed -> `Gregor --notices--> sister.speed @s205`.  NUL-Entity, the expected normal morning is absent -> handled as the s14 entity_transform above (the absence of 'man' is the transform's left side).

significance (DEF, EVA, REC).arity: acts on a reading/state, not primarily on a node-to-node tie
significance (DEF, EVA, REC).DEF_default: the terms are SET. Property line on the thing held: `A: held-as <X> @s` — or entity_transform if a prior reading is being replaced. DEF is the standing prediction; it reads as a state, not an action between two nodes.
significance (DEF, EVA, REC).EVA_default: the reading is TESTED. This is the trigger position — an EVA that fails is what licenses a transform. Emit entity_transform or relational_transform showing the state that broke: `A: read-as X ==> found Y`. If EVA confirms (no break), it may produce only a low-confidence property line or nothing (the model didn't move).
significance (DEF, EVA, REC).REC_default: the frame RESETS. Always a transform — entity_transform for a single reading recast (`A: X ==> Y`), or relational_transform if a whole tie/field re-vectors (`A <--> B: X ==> Y`). REC is where the largest ==> arcs live. After-state [unnameable] if unwitnessed.
significance (DEF, EVA, REC).worked: EVA-Lens, the family's reading of Gregor-as-ill is tested and fails -> `Gregor: read-as ill ==> taken-as other (==> s201)`.  REC-Paradigm, the household frame regrounds -> `household <--> Gregor: centered-on ==> turned-away (==> s700+)`.

ARROW_CONSTRUCTION.stamp_rule: Ongoing state true at/through the cursor: (s<first>+, current). Point event: @s<idx>. Transform spanning two points: (s<left> ==> s<right>). The stamp is built from the event's s-index/indices, never invented.
ARROW_CONSTRUCTION.void_rule: Any slot the text does not witness is [void] (a missing endpoint) or [unnameable] (an unwitnessed after-state of a transform). NEVER fill a void with a guess. The void IS the honest note.
ARROW_CONSTRUCTION.override_rule: note_rel is the cell's generic relation label (CON-Link -> 'binds'). When the graph carries a more specific relation for this edge (tends, loves, depends-on, advocates-for), USE IT instead of the generic. The cell guarantees a label exists; the graph refines it.
ARROW_CONSTRUCTION.self_check: (1) every note has a stamp; (2) every --> points agent->patient; (3) no relation label is an EO code; (4) no ==> right-state postdates the block cursor; (5) unresolved slots are [void]/[unnameable], not guessed; (6) Desert (SYN-Field) never emits — misfire -> demote.

_meta.cursor_scoping_replaces_guards: A notes block for a query at cursor C contains ONLY edges/transforms whose LEFT-state is true at C. No post-C state is ever emitted, so there is nothing to forbid and no guard is written. At s205, Grete is `sister --tends--> Gregor (s330+, current)`; the `==> rejects` half is simply absent because s665 hasn't been replayed. Self-policing: a `==>` whose right-state s-index postdates the block's cursor is a visible bug. The query sets the cursor — a 'how does X change over the book' question is asked at end-of-text, so its block legitimately contains the full `==>` arc.
_meta.storage_vs_wire: Edges are stored as structs {src, rel, tgt, s, conf, type} and operated on by the surprise ranker and cursor-scoper as fields. The arrow-string is PRESENTATION, serialized only into the talker's prompt. Do not store arrows; store fields. (Same reason you don't store dates as display strings.)
_meta.attested_verbs_role: Corpus verbs (make/say/run = INS-Entity; bind/tie/join = CON-Link; ...) are the cell CLASSIFIER anchor: they decide which cell a clause lands in. They are not output. note_rel and fold_verb are the output, in plain language. provenance flags which cells have real attested inventories vs extrapolated banks awaiting the Drive exemplars.
_meta.voice_wall: talker gets notes (arrows + plain relation labels); user gets fold (prose). Neither gets cell keys, attested_verbs, EO codes, or 'surprise'/'profile'.
_meta.selection_rule: Band first (model-movement magnitude) -> indexes fold_verb and the confidence tag. note_rel is usually band-invariant (the relation is the relation); band shows up in the tag and the fold verb.
_meta.data_source: Attested verbs from EO wiki classification tables. Full ~100-clause/cell exemplars in an unfetchable Drive folder; extrapolated cells await it.

NUL_Unraveling_Kind.arrow: <the kind> : has no case under it @s

fold_verb.high: is fixed as a thing the text keeps returning to

INS_Making_Entity._note: DENSEST cell (16.3%). Most reliable inventory of all 27.

INS_Composing_Kind.arrow: vermin : established as what he now is @s14

fold_verb.low: is set up as a type
fold_verb.high: becomes a kind in its own right

CON_Binding_Link._note: DENSEST structural cell (19.6%). Most narrative ties live here. note_rel is the generic; a specific relation word (tends, loves, depends-on) should override it from the graph when available.

fold_verb.high: becomes one system, more than its parts

DEF_Clearing_Atmosphere.arrow: the-house : holds <Gregor is ill> as the climate @s198

fold_verb.mid: sets the terms of what reads as obvious
fold_verb.high: fixes the whole climate of meaning

DEF_Unraveling_Paradigm.arrow: the-family : upholds <he can be cared for> under strain @s

fold_verb.mid: is held in place under strain
fold_verb.high: is held even as it cracks
shifts the whole sense of what matters

EVA_Binding_Lens.arrow: Gregor : read-as ill ==> taken-as other (==> s201)

EVA_Tracing_Paradigm.arrow: the-care-frame : strains case by case ==> fails (==> s)

fold_verb.high: breaks down under its own cases

fold_verb.mid: resets the terms everything sat under
fold_verb.high: the whole frame comes undone and reforms

REC_Composing_Paradigm._note: ECOLOGY / terminus. Largest frame-level shifts only. ~1%, rare by design.

SITE_NOUNS._doc: Plain noun for the target when no specific graph filler (actual person/bond) is slotted. Never a mechanism name. Serves both registers.

DESERT._doc: SYN(Making,Field) / (2,+1,2). Corpus finding: NO verbs in any language. No note_rel, no fold_verb. If the classifier routes here, treat as misfire and demote. The empty cell is positive evidence for the framework.

WORKED_EXAMPLE_s205._doc: Same edges, two registers. Notes are serialized graph to the talker; fold is prose to the user. Block is cursor-scoped to s205 — no post-s205 state present, so no guard.
WORKED_EXAMPLE_s205.fold_user_facing: Right here the family realizes something is badly wrong with Gregor — the chief clerk has heard his voice as an animal's, his mother is screaming, his father is shouting for a locksmith. In the rush his sister has dressed and come running, and Gregor, oddly, notices how fast she managed it. The text gives no reason for the quickness — it's just his attention catching on something ordinary while everything falls apart. (His sister is the closest figure to him here; she's still the one looking after him.)

WORKED_EXAMPLE_whole_book._doc: Query 'what is this about' is asked at end-of-text, so the cursor is the end and the full ==> arcs are legitimately present. Notes carry the reversals; fold names them.
notes_wire: sister <--> Gregor: tends ==> rejects (s330+ ==> s665)   [reversal, high]
household <--> Gregor: centered-on ==> turned-away (==> s700+)   [turning point]
WORKED_EXAMPLE_whole_book.fold_user_facing: It's about Gregor Samsa, who wakes one morning transformed into an insect, and what that does to his family over the months that follow. The change is never undone or explained — the book is really about everyone around it. His sister begins as the one who looks after him and slowly becomes the one insisting they get rid of him. His father, broken and dependent at the start, ends up back at work and back in command. By the end the household has quietly turned back toward its own life, closing on the parents realizing their daughter has grown up. The horror isn't the transformation; it's how the family reorganizes around it and, finally, past it.
